comments.author: @georgecoetzee
comments.text: I thought that they were pure Caucasians.

comments.author: @sandcastlelyndylynneklynch766
comments.text:
Oh, And it is VERY RUDE TO TAKE PICTURES OR VIDEOS of them! To tbem Photos are "Graven Image" which God says in the Holy Bible "Do not have any Graven Images.." They do not put faces on their rag dolls for the children! They used to not allow a mirror,  but now only for a quick hair comb or shave above the beard. Men wear beards when they get married w out any .mustaches! 
 But dont take pictures of them!  you might want to go up, introduce yourself and then ask if you may get a photo of them from the side profile or backs! They would rather you talk to them than talk loudly about them right in front of them!! Do not be rude!! Most of us are very protective of the Amish, But there is always a rude person or a bully or someone who is innocent enough but not educated! 
Blessings!!

comments.author: @sandcastlelyndylynneklynch766
comments.text:
In my state of Ohio, USA!! 
 I live quite away from Holmes Couny. Which is in North Central Ohio!
I live in Dayton Ohio,  the invention capital of the World, in Southwest Ohio! 
It is 154 miles away from Millersberg which is the County seat in Holmes! It wld take me 2 hours and 49 minutes to drive North East to get there!
When I was a little girl My dad got me an Amish Story book for children, about a boy named Jonathan Lapp! I loved the sketched illustrations so much! At that time,  I had already read the whole series by Laura Ingles Wilder! And
Around that same time, the TV series Little House and The Prairie had a movie about Little House in the Big Woods, then the TV series! 
So I felt the Amish were the "modern" version of living a Prairie life?! Haha I wanted to be an Amish girl!! Time passed, I grew up.. got married, had 2 daughters, 
When I was in my 30s I found the author Wanda Brunstedder she was a former Amish person and she wrote beautiful love stories and very real settings of Amish Life!!  Even their way of not wanting to interact w the "English" so much! They speak their own dialect "Pennsylvania Dutch" and "The English" is everyone else in the world who isn't Amish! They didn't care if you were from France, Lithuania or The Congo! Even speaking other languages than English, if you weren't Amish you were "Engljsh"!!  They like to be "simple" 
 And they only go to school the required amount in America, which I guess is 8th grade?! But I know if I or my daughters skipped any school in high school or were absent sick long in high school they wld call the Truant officer to go look for you! Haha I never understood how they only got to go until 8th grade??! 
 Anyways, my point of rambling on so much, is that I have always loved the Amish people!! Great Builders! Great cooks and hosts too! We have Amish near us about 20 mins away, in Germantown,  and Waynesville and their furniture, and restaurants are great! They also have a "Guesthoff" hotel which is lovely too!!
In Indiana USA,  there is the Swish Amish and there is a darling village out there!! 
Great video
Blessings
